input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:

Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText: The user is asking about a behavioral difference between VSTest and Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP). The issue is that MTP returns exit code 8 when a test assembly contains zero tests, whereas VSTest treated this as a success. Let me look at their test project to understand the setup.

data.content:
Exit code 8 in Microsoft.Testing.Platform means **zero tests discovered**, which it treats as a failure by default (VSTest didn't). Since `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` isn't defined, all tests are compiled out.

The fix is the `--minimum-expected-tests 0` option. Let me verify and apply it:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests to see exit code 8

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests to see exit code 8

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. With Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP), exit code 8 means "no tests were found." When `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` is not defined, the entire test class is compiled out, resulting in zero tests. MTP treats this as an error (exit code 8), whereas VSTest would return 0.

The fix is to set the MSBuild property `<TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>false</TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>` — no wait, that's not the right one.

Let me think about the correct property. In Microsoft.Testing.Platform, exit code 8 is `ZeroTests`. The way to handle this is:


I can either pass `--minimum-expected-tests 0` as a command-line argument to the test runner, or set the corresponding MSBuild property to suppress the zero tests error. Since we're running tests in a CI pipeline, the command-line option is probably the most straightforward approach. Adding the property to the .csproj file... I'm not entirely sure if that MSBuild property actually exists, so I should probably just try building and running the project to see what error I get, then apply the appropriate fix based on what happens.

data.content: Confirmed — 0 total tests causes a failure. Now let me apply the fix:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Allow assemblies with zero tests (e.g. conditional compilation) to pass -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

The exit code from `dotnet test` is 1. Let me check the actual test runner's exit code. Let me look at the log file to confirm the zero tests issue. But the output clearly shows `Failed: 0, Passed: 0, Skipped: 0, Total: 0` — zero tests discovered, treated as failure.

Now let me apply the fix. I'll add `--minimum-expected-tests 0` to the project. Let me try the approach:

data.content: Found it! `--minimum-expected-tests` requires a **non-zero** positive integer — `0` is rejected. But there's `--ignore-exit-code 8` which is exactly what we need:

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Allow assemblies with zero tests (e.g. conditional compilation) to pass -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Allow assemblies with zero tests (e.g. conditional compilation) to pass -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText:
The error is clear:
```
Option '--minimum-expected-tests' has invalid arguments: '--minimum-expected-tests' expects a single non-zero positive integer value
```

So `--minimum-expected-tests 0` doesn't accept 0! It expects a non-zero positive integer.

But I also see another option in the help:
```
--ignore-exit-code
    Do not report non successful exit value for specific exit codes
    (e.g. '--ignore-exit-code 8;9' ignore exit code 8 and 9 and will return 0 in these case)
```

So the fix is to use `--ignore-exit-code 8` instead. Exit code 8 is the "zero tests" exit code. 

I'll apply that approach.

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Allow assemblies with zero tests (e.g. conditional compilation) to pass -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Allow assemblies with zero tests (e.g. conditional compilation) to pass -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

<!-- Allow assemblies with zero tests (e.g. conditional compilation) to pass -->

toolArgs.new_str:
<!-- Allow assemblies with zero tests (e.g. conditional compilation) to pass -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content: Both scenarios work. Here's the final file:

toolCalls.name: view
toolCalls.args: {"path": "/tmp/sv-9470d07ea1f84c77ba0d8e13806053ea/TestProject.csproj"}

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.     <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
5.     <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
6.     <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
7.     <!-- Allow assemblies with zero tests (e.g. conditional compilation) to pass -->
8.     <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
9.   </PropertyGroup>
10.   <ItemGroup>
11.     <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
12.   </ItemGroup>
13. </Project>
14.

     <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
     <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
     <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
     <!-- Allow assemblies with zero tests (e.g. conditional compilation) to pass -->
     <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

   </ItemGroup>

data.content:
**The fix:** Add `--ignore-exit-code 8` via `<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>` in your `.csproj`.

**What happened:** Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns exit code **8** when zero tests are discovered (unlike VSTest which returned 0). The `--ignore-exit-code 8` option tells the runner to treat that specific exit code as success, while still correctly failing on actual test failures.
